reviews.review_msg: An inspiring  movie or documentary before most of the lectures.The tests are quite manageable, if you pay attention to the lecture slides, and also to the textbook. At the end of the course, he curved up the final exam a lot. Nice prof.

You're marked on 2 exams and 1 small paper. 1/3 of lectures have a BBC video, sometimes with David Attenborough. He says that exam content is focused on concepts rather than random facts but he didn't do that with the final exam. Thankfully, he bumped everyone's final exam mark by 14%. Pay attention in class and take notes and you'll be fine.
He is a funny and geed prof, however, the midterm exam and the final are super hard, you must read the textbook and pay attention to lectures in order to pass this course.

I took ARCH 131 with Mark last Summer to fulfill a Breadth requirement. Hes an awesome prof. Very funny and personable. I have now fulfilled an extended minor in Arch because Mark made me want to continue taking Arch classes. The course was comprised of 4 exams, all multiple choice. Go to lectures and study and youll do well.

Mark is a really good prof and I don't really see why there are negative reviews on him. I took this course as intersession and Mark makes the 3hour lectures quite bearable. The exams are so simple and he gives up to 10 marks of bonus on the final. Very engaging and approachable.

Lots of reading and assignments based off the reading. But at least it's online articles and he chose an affordable text. Interesting material, clearly his passion, too much focus on defining the field/its reception for my taste. Fair and quick grader, good feedback, class discussions so do your homework every week to get a good grade.

he's a super funny guy, really nice, and you could tell he was really passionate about his field.. but the class was REALLY hard.. i took it as an elective and regret it.. the exams were super hard and there was a TON of readings. thank god he scaled the exams or else i probably would have failed :/

Mark is obviously very passionate about human origins. I think he would be a great teacher for an upper level course but NOT for a 100 level course. Grading was harsh and tests were hard, each one had to be scaled. If you do take this course GO TO LECTURE and take diligent notes. Don't take this course unless you are interested  in human origins.

This is an example of a prof who shouldn't be teaching 100 level classes. He is obviously very passionate, but impossible to keep up with as a beginner, and his tests/marking rhetoric are very hard and inconsistent. The course was trying to do too much, and in the process he has likely turned a lot of eager students off of human origins.

I strongly, STRONGLY, advise others not to take this course. Even when you study hard it's extremely difficult to get a good grade. It was never clear what we should study. Some questions on the exams seemed to come out of nowhere, and thats WITH studying my butt off!  Very difficult, certainly challenged me...
